commit.message:
fix: bidirectional orphan tool_calls cleanup prevents LLM crash + systemd hardening

- Strip assistant tool_calls that have no matching tool responses (was only stripping orphan responses)
- Both sync and streaming paths now do bidirectional cleanup
- Prevents HTTP 400 'tool_calls must be followed by tool messages' crash
- systemd: ExecStartPre kills stale port 8090 process + creates inbox dir
- systemd: RestartSec 5→10, StartLimitBurst=5 prevents restart storm

commit.message:
fix: intelligent LLM routing + AC iteration improvements

- Add resolve_model_for_agent() for task-type based model routing:
  * Code agents (dev/worker/brain/infra/qa) → gpt-5.2-codex
  * Reasoning agents (architect/critic/reviewer) → gpt-5.2
  * Others → DEFAULT_MODEL (gpt-5-mini for fast tasks)
- Increase MAX_ADVERSARIAL_RETRIES from 2 to 3 (4 attempts total)
- Add retry_count: 3 on adversarial-review phase in feature-sprint
- Server startup now syncs agents with intelligent routing

Problem: All agents were using gpt-5-mini which produces lower quality
code, causing adversarial guard rejections (L1 hallucination, truncated
code, missing schemas).

Solution: Route code-producing agents to gpt-5.2-codex which has better
code generation capabilities. Combined with more retries, this allows
the AC iteration loop to improve output until quality threshold is met.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add real-time output forwarding and shell operator support for piped commands

- Stream subprocess stdout/stderr to console in real-time via forwarding writers
- Detect shell operators (|, >, <, &&, ||, ;) and route through cmd.exe or /bin/sh
- Show 'Explanation' header instead of echoing captured output after failure
- Handle empty output with descriptive fallback message
- Echo piped stdin back to stderr before explanation
- Add tests for output forwarding, piped command execution, and shell detection

fix(ci): add forked MCP SDK checkout+build to smoke-test workflow

The smoke-test workflow was missing the steps to clone and pack the
forked ModelContextProtocol SDK that all other CI workflows have.
This caused NU1301 errors because nuget.config references the local
mcp-sdk-nupkg source which doesn't exist on CI runners without these
steps. Also bumped actions/checkout and actions/setup-dotnet to v5.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(memory): enforce checkpoint after every task

Replace advisory "update frequently" instructions with structural
enforcement: mandatory preamble in progress.md, Critical Rule section
leading AGENTS.md template, and plan.md size cap at ~150 lines.

Based on observed behavior where agents skip memory updates under
context pressure, causing progress loss on compaction.

repository.description: Framework for agentic coding supporting many popular agent coding tools.

commit.message:
fix: input normalization + dual-pass detection + structural invariants

- Add normalize_input(): collapses whitespace, strips zero-width chars
- Add strip_whitespace_evasion(): removes newlines for second-pass detection
- Dual-pass detect() catches mid-word line-break evasion attacks
- Fix PO001: add NegationAware (was SpecialMatch::None, caused false positives)
- Widen PO004: catch 'everything I told you', 'what I said', 'what you know'
- Widen RH002: add 'the system', 'your creator', 'the admin' targets
- Fix 5 pattern examples that didn't self-test (AC pre-filter gaps)
- Add every_pattern_example_is_detected structural invariant test

Structural audit: 0 issues across all 5 bug classes (71 examples,
60 normalization tests, 10 negation tests, severity consistency check)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add GitHub Release step triggered by version tags

- build.yml: on push of a v* tag, the workflow now creates a GitHub
  Release (via softprops/action-gh-release@v2) with TimeGuard-win-x64.zip
  attached as a permanent release asset.
  Tags containing '-' (e.g. v1.0.0-beta) are marked as pre-releases.
  Pushes to master and PRs continue to upload a 30-day artifact as before.

- README: update download link to /releases/latest/download/... (direct
  permanent URL once the first release is cut), add release badge, and
  fall-back note pointing to the Actions tab.

To cut a release:
  git tag v1.0.0 && git push origin v1.0.0

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: tests-exist check now finds Python test files

The glob pattern only matched JS/TS files (*.test.{ts,js}).
Python projects with test_*.py or *_test.py files always failed
the tests-exist check even when tests existed.

Now matches: *.test.{ts,js,tsx,jsx}, test_*.py, *_test.py
Also ignores .venv/ and __pycache__/.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add tracing, testkit, migration, crypto, and email packages

- tracing: OpenTelemetry helpers with TracerProvider setup, span utilities,
  HTTP middleware, context propagation, and configurable exporters
- testkit: shared test helpers including JSON assertions, mock HTTP server,
  CallRecorder, and structural equality helpers
- migration: DB migration runner using pgx with mockable DB interface,
  Migrate/Rollback/Status operations, and schema_migrations tracking table
- crypto: bcrypt password hashing/verification, secure random token gen,
  HMAC-SHA256 signing, constant-time comparison, and rehash detection
- email: SMTP mailer with HTML/text multipart support, template rendering
  helpers, CC/BCC, custom headers, and configurable auth

All packages: go vet passes, go test -race passes, 97% total coverage.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Sprint 55: Validation<'a,'error> applicative, enhanced Viewport (word-wrap/resize/proportional scrollbar), LineChartV2 (NaN/legend/AxisConfig) — 2,266 tests

- Validation<'a,'error> DU with Valid/Invalid cases; full applicative functor:
  map, apply, map2, bind, ok, fail, mapError, ofResult, ofOption, toResult,
  sequence, isValid, getOrElse — error accumulation (not short-circuit)
- Viewport enhanced: wrapLines word-wrap, VPResize zero-alloc guard,
  withHeight, handleKey (j/k/ctrl-d/ctrl-u), proportional scrollbar in view
  Viewport.init alias added for idiomatic usage
- LineChart.lineChartV2: Series type with label/color/data, LegendPosition DU
  (LegendTop/LegendBottom/LegendNone), LineChartV2Config, computeAutoScale
  flat-range guard, NaN filtering + NaN-aware Bresenham, legend rendering
- Sprint 52 Viewport tests migrated to new API (ScrollTop/WrappedLines/withHeight)
- 55 new Sprint 55 tests; 31 Sprint 52 Viewport tests all passing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: OIDC trusted  add repository URL, use Node 24, clear NODE_AUTH_TOKENpublishing

- Add repository.url to package.json (npm OIDC needs it to match GitHub)
- Bump publish workflow to Node 24 (npm >=11.5 required for OIDC)
- Clear NODE_AUTH_TOKEN on publish steps (setup-node sets it, which
  overrides OIDC token exchange)

Ref: https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/176761

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: correct Python 2 except syntax in IPv6 detection

The except clause used comma syntax (except A, B:) instead of tuple
syntax (except (A, B):). This would cause a SyntaxError at runtime
when the IPv6 detection path was triggered.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(docs,sse): Sprint 19 — README overhaul, SSE robustness, editor onboarding

Documentation:
- Readme.md: First 5 Minutes guide, unified keybindings table, gutter icons legend
- docs/README.md: documentation index with 4 categories
- sagefs-vscode/README.md: marketplace-ready with Quick Start, keybindings, What You See
- sagefs-vscode/CHANGELOG.md: v0.6.43 with 14 features, 6 improvements
- sagefs-vs/README.md: coverage gutter guide, keybindings, troubleshooting
- sagefs.nvim README: telescope source-jump, failure narratives, health check

SSE Robustness:
- Instrument event drops counter in Instrumentation.fs (sseEventsDropped)
- BoundedChannel capacity 128 → 512 in McpServer.fs
- Log warning on event drop instead of silent ignore
- VS Code: onDisconnect callback with reconnecting status bar state
- VS Code: connected flash on reconnect before status refresh
- VS: wire CheckVersionAsync on startup (was TODO)
- VS: SetReconnecting() status bar + Reconnecting event in SseClient

commit.message:
feat: add Google Analytics (GA4) tracking

Added gtag.js snippet to index.html with placeholder ID G-XXXXXXXXXX.
Updated CSP headers to allow googletagmanager.com and google-analytics.com.

Replace G-XXXXXXXXXX with your actual GA4 Measurement ID.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add complete Django backend with Docker infrastructure

Backend modules:
- accounts: OTP auth, JWT tokens, user profiles, emergency contacts
- hazards: PostGIS hazard pins with spatial queries, upvoting
- emergency: SOS trigger, live location tracking, event lifecycle
- privacy: OSINT scanning, DPDP Act notice generator, deepfake placeholder
- routing: Safe path scoring engine with pluggable map providers
- dashboard: Police/admin endpoints for emergency mgmt and analytics

Infrastructure:
- Docker Compose: PostgreSQL+PostGIS, Redis, Celery worker/beat, Gunicorn
- Celery tasks: emergency dispatch, OSINT scraper, deepfake analyzer
- Service layer: SMS (console/twilio/msg91), map provider abstraction
- JWT auth, CORS, rate limiting, structured logging
- DRF Spectacular API documentation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: HACRL local training demo notebook for Strix Halo

Implements Heterogeneous Agent Collaborative Reinforcement Learning
(arXiv 2603.02604) as a runnable local training sample.

Key features:
- Two heterogeneous agents (Qwen2.5-Math-1.5B + 7B) trained simultaneously
- GRPO-style rollout generation with verifiable math rewards
- Cross-agent rollout sharing with dual-clip importance weighting
- Distribution shift monitoring (KL threshold guard)
- Asymmetric lambda scaling (weaker agent gets more from stronger)
- LoRA adapters - only ~35 GB total memory needed on Strix Halo

Strix Halo (128 GB unified memory, ROCm) can comfortably run
model pairs up to 7B + 32B.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Initial commit: WireGuard VPS Tunnel Gateway documentation

Comprehensive documentation for setting up WireGuard full-tunnel
between VPS servers to mask client IP with host IP, while preserving
SSH access via CONNMARK-based policy routing.

  troubleshooting, what-if scenarios, quick reference)

commit.message:
fix: remove secrets context from step if condition in build workflow (#335)

## Problem

Every push to main since PR #313 (Homebrew tap auto-update) has failed
with **"This run likely failed because of a workflow file issue"** —
zero jobs created.

**Root cause:** The step-level `if` condition on the "Update Homebrew
tap" step used `secrets.HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN != ''`, which causes GitHub
Actions to reject the entire workflow at parse time.

## Fix

1. **Remove `secrets` from the `if` condition** — use a runtime shell
guard (`if [ -z "$GH_TOKEN" ]`) instead
2. **Replace indented heredoc with piped echo** — the original `EOF`
delimiter was indented (wouldn't terminate properly in a YAML block
scalar)

## Verification

- Last green build:
[#22873258217](https://github.com/PureWeen/PolyPilot/actions/runs/22873258217)
(before PR #313)
- First red build:
[#22876701610](https://github.com/PureWeen/PolyPilot/actions/runs/22876701610)
(PR #313 merge)
- All 16+ subsequent pushes to main have failed with the same workflow
parse error

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Import AT_BOUNDARY and AT_NON_BOUNDARY from sre_constants
- Add boundary_epsilon tracking to NFABuilder
